dimensions.rationale: The request and receipt hashes match the supplied values, Git resolves the candidate to the stated tree, and the receipt is limited to LOCAL_SYNTHETIC_ONLY with no protected execution or remote writes.

Receipt4 contains 15 unique zero-exit candidate-bound commands with checked sanitized-output digests; prior requests, receipts and replies retain their supplied hashes, while the v2 schema, validator and adversarial tests require five ordered attempts per family, ten distinct tasks, two distinct final replies and complete finding closure.

Absent, malformed, stale, failing or incomplete assurance selects only PRE_CLOSURE; v1.0.5 validation additionally requires deterministic equality and clean Git-bound assurance. Protected Oracles, production readiness, publication and final Acceptance remain explicitly blocked rather than inferred from local evidence.

The reviewed execution made no remote writes and removed its ephemeral image; the immutable candidate is the Git rollback boundary, and post-review changes to workflows, runtime, tests, schemas, status authority or package authority are rejected.

findings.finding: Candidate, request, receipt, reply, model-family and distinct platform-task identities are mechanically represented across the five-attempt provenance design.
findings.resolution_ref: The schema and validator require five exact ordered attempts for each of two model families, ten distinct task IDs, common final candidate/request/receipt bindings and distinct final reply artifacts.

findings.finding: Local gate evidence is bound to the exact candidate and carries the complete required command set, zero exit codes, tool versions, raw metadata and verified sanitized-output digests.
findings.resolution_ref: Receipt4 has SHA-256 3359865176269e7eb1da39aa38d6fc9e8230e530f8d02a25f3d8b5f4a3057fc2 and the validator rejects stale subjects, missing or duplicate command IDs, nonzero exits, digest drift, local-path leakage and sensitive patterns.

findings.finding: Effectful writes require prospective repository, LFS, object and release-asset demand, with cumulative reservation before callback execution.
findings.resolution_ref: CapacityPolicy evaluates projected demand, OperationalGate rejects absent or unsafe projections and retains reservations across uncertain failures, while PRODUCTION_RUN is also fail-closed for UNKNOWN or RED capacity.

findings.finding: Post-review integrity covers all workflows and the reviewed runtime, dependency, test, evidence-capture, assurance, status and package authority surfaces.
findings.resolution_ref: The protected prefix set includes the full workflow scope, capture tool, delivery-status model/build/validation paths, governance and package builders/validators, schemas, runtime and tests; adversarial Git cases confirm material drift blocks closure.

findings.finding: The prior root-detected history/composition defects remain repaired, and the delivery-status schema now couples each package version to its complete finding, blocker and next-action state so both crossed combinations are invalid.
findings.resolution_ref: The two root oneOf alternatives bind the full v1.0.4 pre-closure and v1.0.5 closed states; negative tests reject both crossings, and production composition hash-binds the capacity, Kill, gate, runtime and decisive regression surfaces.

findings.finding: The deterministic status model and builder are candidate-complete and cannot select the closed state without a passing hash-checked five-attempt, two-family provenance chain; closed validation also enforces clean Git-bound post-review integrity.
findings.resolution_ref: Only assurance status PASS selects CLOSED; every other result selects PRE_CLOSURE, deterministic validation rejects mismatched status, and v1.0.5 additionally requires the exact review chain with no protected post-candidate drift or dirty worktree.

limitations: This read-only review inspected source and the hash-bound sanitized local receipt but did not execute protected Oracles or independently replay its raw logs; local evidence does not prove real Gmail or private-repository behavior, production health, deployment, publication, RMD-06, RMD-07 or final AC-033. Repository hashes and platform task IDs still require the retained Codex audit log and are not a signed third-party attestation.
summary: PASS: all four dimensions pass, the complete Sol finding history plus RMD05-CLOSURE-002 and RMD05-CLOSURE-003 is resolved, and no open finding remains within the strictly local read-only review scope.
